nativeRange.habitat: Passes and outer reef slopes at depths of 1–30 m; typically associated with rocky rubble and coral reefs

diet.notes: Tends to accept a wide range of aquarium foods including vitamin-enriched frozen brine shrimp, bloodworms, glassworms, as well as flake and pellet foods. In the wild has been documented feeding on algae and small zooplankton. Generally advised to feed 2–3 times daily.

goodWith: gobies and blennies of similar size
avoidWith: other small damsels unless in large tanks with ample territories

summary: A vivid canary-yellow damselfish from the outer reefs of the Cook Islands and Polynesia. Generally considered easy to keep and reef-compatible, though tends to be semi-aggressive toward similar-sized fish in smaller aquariums.
careNotes: The Canary Deep Water Damsel is collected from outer reef passes and slopes at depths commonly exceeding 45 m (150 ft) in the wild, which likely contributes to its vivid yellow coloration and relative scarcity in the hobby. Despite the 'deep water' common name, it adapts well to standard aquarium conditions. LiveAquaria describes it as a mild-mannered damsel relative to other damsels, though it is still classified semi-aggressive and has been known to harass smaller or more passive tankmates in confined spaces. A minimum tank of around 200 L is generally advised to provide adequate territory, with plenty of rockwork and hiding places. Temperature: cross-source disagreement exists — LiveAquaria recommends 72–78°F (22.2–25.6°C) while FishBase models the wild range as 23.2–29°C; an averaged range of 22.5–27.5°C is used here, with standard reef conditions of 24–26°C being broadly acceptable. Salinity: standard reef specific gravity of 1.020–1.025. This species is documented as oviparous with distinct pairing during breeding; males guard and aerate the demersal egg clutches, typical of Pomacentridae. Lifespan data is not available in sources consulted.
breedingNotes: Has been reported to form distinct pairs during breeding. Males have been documented guarding and aerating demersal egg clutches attached to substrate, consistent with Pomacentridae breeding patterns. Captive breeding data is not available in sources consulted.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Species-specific product page found via site search (collection URL redirected to no listing; product page at /products/canary-deep-water-damsel). Provided: size (3.5 in), temperature (72–78°F), specific gravity (1.020–1.025), pH (8.1–8.4), minimum tank (50 gal), temperament (semi-aggressive), care level (easy), diet (omnivore), reef compatibility (yes).

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-verified scientific name, family (Pomacentridae), order (Ovalentaria), native range (Cook Islands, Austral Islands, Rapa, Gambier, Pitcairn groups), maximum size (7.0 cm SL), temperature range (23.2–29°C modeled), depth range (1–30 m), diet (algae and plankton), and reproductive mode (oviparous, distinct pairing, male guards eggs). Temperature disagreement: FishBase upper bound (29°C) is higher than LiveAquaria (25.6°C); averaged to 27.5°C. FishBase does not list pH or salinity values.

fish.breedingNotes: Males guard and aerate demersal egg clutches; distinct pairing observed. No captive breeding data available.
